questions.text: Radiotherapy used in the treatment of cancer usually use gamma-rays from:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: a) Copper-60:Copper-60 is not typically used in radiotherapy for the treatment of cancer. It is a radioactive isotope of copper, but its gamma-ray emission is not widely employed in medical treatments.

Cobalt-60 emits two high energy gamma rays, making cobalt-60 both an internal and external hazard. The primary exposure pathways of concern are ingestion (drinking water and fish consumption), and exposure by inhalation and external exposure.

c) Gold:Gold is not used as a gamma-ray source for cancer radiotherapy. It is used in some medical applications, such as in the form of gold markers for imaging purposes, but not as a treatment radiation source.
d) Silver:Silver is also not used as a gamma-ray source for cancer radiotherapy. It is used for various purposes in medicine, but it is not employed for cancer treatment using gamma rays.

questions.explanation: Cobalt-60 emits two high energy gamma rays, making cobalt-60 both an internal and external hazard. The primary exposure pathways of concern are ingestion (drinking water and fish consumption), and exposure by inhalation and external exposure.

questions.text: The wavelength of beta rays is measured by the _.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Interference is a phenomenon that occurs when two or more waves meet and combine. While beta rays can exhibit wave-like behavior and interfere with each other, interference is not a direct method of measuring their wavelength.
Polarization is the phenomenon where the oscillation of waves is restricted to a specific direction. It is not directly used for measuring the wavelength of beta rays.

Absorption is the process by which the energy of beta rays is absorbed by a material as they pass through it. While absorption spectroscopy can provide information about the energy levels of beta rays, it is not the primary method for measuring their wavelength.

Beta rays are high-speed electrons or positrons that are emitted from the nucleus during beta decay. These electrons or positrons have a characteristic wavelength associated with their motion, known as the de Broglie wavelength. Diffraction is a phenomenon in which waves bend around an obstacle and interfere with each other. When beta rays are diffracted through a crystal lattice, the diffraction pattern produced can be used to determine the wavelength of the beta rays. Therefore, the wavelength of beta rays is measured by diffraction, making option D the correct answer.

questions.explanation: Beta rays are high-speed electrons or positrons that are emitted from the nucleus during beta decay. These electrons or positrons have a characteristic wavelength associated with their motion, known as the de Broglie wavelength. Diffraction is a phenomenon in which waves bend around an obstacle and interfere with each other. When beta rays are diffracted through a crystal lattice, the diffraction pattern produced can be used to determine the wavelength of the beta rays. Therefore, the wavelength of beta rays is measured by diffraction, making option D the correct answer.

questions.text: The existence of positron was discovered in the:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: An experiment on Cosmic rays performed by Carl D Anderson led to the accidental discovery of the positron. Anderson allowed cosmic rays to pass through a cloud chamber and a lead plate. A magnet surrounded this apparatus, caused the particles to bend in different directions based on their electric charge. The ion trail left by each positron appeared on the photographic plate with a curvature matching the mass-to-charge ratio of an electron, but in a direction that showed its charge was positive. It was the first evidence of anti-matter.

questions.text: The artifacts and fossils are used to estimate ages by measuring _ content.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: a) mineral:Option (a) "mineral" is not the correct answer. While minerals play a role in certain dating methods, they are not the primary factor used to estimate ages in artifacts and fossils.

b) chemical:Option (b) "chemical" is not the correct answer on its own. Chemical analysis can be an important part of some dating techniques, but it is not the only factor considered in estimating ages.

One of the most widely known radiometric dating techniques, radiocarbon dating measures the decay of the radioactive isotope Carbon-14 (C-14) in any organic material found in archaeological deposits, such as wood, plants, textiles, and human or animal remains to determine its age.

d) all of these:Option (d) "all of these" is not the correct answer. While some dating methods may involve aspects of minerals, chemicals, and radioactive isotopes, not all methods use all three simultaneously. The primary method used to estimate ages in artifacts and fossils is through radioactive dating.

questions.text: Which is not radioactive?

options.explanation: O3 is the molecular formula of ozone, and it is formed when UV light interacts with O2 present in the atmosphere. Hence, it is not radioactive.

Hydrogen has an isotope (tritium) that is radioactive, but it is very rare. Protium is the commonly present isotope of hydrogen and it is not radioactive. Hence, we can say that hydrogen is not radioactive.
An isotope of sodium, Sodium-24, is radioactive. However, normally present sodium is not radioactive. Hence, we can say that sodium is not radioactive.

questions.explanation: Ozone, Hydrogen and Sodium are all not radioactive because they have stable nuclei. questions.text: The star which is very hot will emit:

options.explanation: The hottest stars tend to appear blue or blue-white, whereas the coolest stars are red. This is because in the energy spectrum, the lowest energy is of red color and energy increases toward the blue-violet color.
Red stars (e.g., Betelgeuse) are the coolest, with temperatures below 3,500 K.
Orange stars (e.g., Aldebaran) are cooler than blue ones, with temperatures around 3,500 – 5,000 K.
While a star can emit green light, it also emits other colors, making it appear white rather than purely green.

questions.text: Which one of the following statements is correct?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Legumes fix nitrogen only through the specialized bacteria that live in their roots.
options.explanation: Yes, that's correct. Legumes, such as soybeans, peas, and clover, have a mutualistic relationship with specialized bacteria called rhizobia. These rhizobia colonize the roots of legume plants and form nodules, where they can convert atmospheric nitrogen (N2) into a form that can be used by the plant. This mutualistic relationship between legumes and rhizobia is a significant ecological and agricultural benefit. It reduces the need for synthetic nitrogen fertilizers, which can have negative environmental impacts, and helps improve soil fertility.

options.text: Legumes fix nitrogen independently of the specialized bacteria that live in their roots.
options.explanation: Legumes do not fix nitrogen independently; they rely on the specialized bacteria (rhizobia) that live in their roots for nitrogen fixation. This mutualistic relationship is essential for their ability to convert atmospheric nitrogen (N2) into a usable form (ammonia, NH3) for plant growth.

options.text: Legumes fix nitrogen only through specialized bacteria that live in their leaves.
options.explanation: Legumes fix nitrogen through the specialized bacteria that live in their roots, not in their leaves.

options.text: Legumes are incapable of fixing nitrogen.
options.explanation: Legumes are indeed capable of fixing nitrogen, and they are well-known for their ability to do so through a symbiotic relationship with specialized bacteria known as rhizobia. This mutualistic relationship allows legumes to convert atmospheric nitrogen (N2) into a form that can be used by the plant for its growth and development.

questions.explanation: The nitrogen­-fixing ability of leguminous plants is not a property of the plants as such but results from infection of their roots by bacteria in the soil, leading to the formation of nodules. These organisms are Gram­-negative motile rods that are classified in the genus Rhizobium.

questions.text: The forces of friction causes:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The combination of velocity-dependent friction and variable normal force can lead to a parametrically excited instability. Due to the coupling of the oscillating normal force and the velocity-dependent friction, a parametric damping term arises in the equations of motion in the transverse direction. Friction can slow things down and stop stationary things from moving. In a frictionless world, more objects would be sliding about, clothes and shoes would be difficult to keep on and it would be very difficult for people or cars to get moving or change direction.
Friction causes excitations but it doesn't cause de-excitation.
Friction doesn't cause magnetization.
This option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Option: A The combination of velocity-dependent friction and variable normal force can lead to parametrically excited instability. Due to the coupling of the oscillating normal force and the velocity-dependent friction, a parametric damping term arises in the equations of motion in the transverse direction. Friction can slow things down and stop stationary things from moving. In a frictionless world, more objects would be sliding about, clothes and shoes would be difficult to keep on and it would be very difficult for people or cars to get moving or change direction. Option B : Friction causes excitations but it doesn't cause de-excitation. Option C : Friction doesn't cause magnetization.

questions.text: The deuterium atom has _ quarks
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect. A single nucleon (proton or neutron) contains three quarks, but a deuterium atom consists of more than one nucleon.

Correct. A deuterium atom consists of one proton and one neutron, and each of these contains three quarks:A proton has two up quarks and one down quark (uud).A neutron has one up quark and two down quarks (udd).So, the total number of quarks in deuterium is 3 (proton) + 3 (neutron) = 6 quarks.
Incorrect. This would imply the presence of three nucleons, but deuterium only has two nucleons (one proton and one neutron).
Incorrect. This number would correspond to four nucleons, which is not the case for deuterium.

questions.explanation: Correct. A deuterium atom consists of one proton and one neutron, and each of these contains three quarks:A proton has two up quarks and one down quark (uud).A neutron has one up quark and two down quarks (udd).So, the total number of quarks in deuterium is 3 (proton) + 3 (neutron) = 6 quarks.

questions.text: The process by which a heavy nucleus splits up into lighter nuclei is known as:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Fission occurs when a neutron slams into a larger atom, forcing it to excite and split into two smaller atoms—also known as fission products. Additional neutrons are also released that can initiate a chain reaction.
Nuclear fusion is a reaction in which two or more atomic nuclei, usually deuterium and tritium, are combined to form one or more different atomic nuclei and subatomic particles.
Alpha decay is a nuclear decay process where an unstable nucleus changes to another element by shooting out a particle composed of two protons and two neutrons.
Chain reaction is the self-sustaining fission reaction spread by neutrons which occurs in nuclear reactors and bombs

questions.explanation: Fission occurs when a neutron slams into a larger atom, forcing it to excite and split into two smaller atoms—also known as fission products. Additional neutrons are also released that can initiate a chain reaction. When each atom splits, a tremendous amount of energy is released.

questions.text: For an unknown element X the wavelength recorded is 2.9x10A-2 m, the half life of element is _ years
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect. Wavelength and half-life are independent properties. The half-life cannot be determined from the given wavelength.
Incorrect. The wavelength does not provide information about the half-life. The half-life is specific to the isotope, which is not given here.
Incorrect. The half-life of an element's isotope is independent of its electromagnetic wavelength. Thus, it cannot be deduced from the wavelength.
Correct. The half-life cannot be determined from the wavelength provided, as these properties are unrelated.

questions.explanation: The question presents a scenario where the wavelength of electromagnetic radiation associated with an element X is given, but asks for the half-life of the element. Wavelength is a property related to electromagnetic radiation, such as light, and is not directly connected to the nuclear properties of an element. Half-life, on the other hand, is a nuclear property that defines the time required for half of the radioactive isotope's nuclei to decay. Because the problem does not provide any information about the specific isotope of element X, the half-life cannot be calculated from the wavelength alone. Therefore, the answer is None of these as there is no direct correlation between the given wavelength and the unknown half-life.

questions.text: If two particles collide with one anti particle the resultant will give
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: a) One particle, one antiparticle (Option a):Option (a) "One particle, one antiparticle" is not the correct answer. When a particle collides with its corresponding antiparticle, they can annihilate each other, leading to the release of energy in the form of photons or other particles.

b) Total annihilation (Option b):Option (b) "Total annihilation" is the correct answer. When a particle collides with its corresponding antiparticle, they can annihilate each other, converting their mass energy into other forms of energy, such as photons or other particles. This process is called "annihilation," and it results in the complete conversion of the particles' masses into energy.

c) One antiparticle only (Option c):Option (c) "One antiparticle only" is not the correct answer. In a collision between a particle and its corresponding antiparticle, both the particle and antiparticle are involved in the process of annihilation. They do not survive the collision as separate entities.
d) None of these (Option d):Option (d) "None of these" is not the correct answer either. The correct answer is option b: "Total annihilation," as explained above.

questions.explanation: b) Total annihilation (Option b):Option (b) "Total annihilation" is the correct answer. When a particle collides with its corresponding antiparticle, they can annihilate each other, converting their mass energy into other forms of energy, such as photons or other particles. This process is called "annihilation," and it results in the complete conversion of the particles' masses into energy.

questions.text: The control rods in a nuclear reactor control the reaction by
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Control rods in a nuclear reactor control the reaction by decreasing the speed of neutrons.In a nuclear reactor, the fission process occurs when a neutron collides with uranium orplutonium nucleus, causing it to split into two smaller nuclei and releasing additional neutrons.These released neutrons can go on to collide with other nuclei, resulting in a chain reaction.Control rods, typically made of materials such as boron or cadmium, are inserted into thereactor core to regulate the rate of the chain reaction. These control rods are capable ofabsorbing neutrons, effectively reducing their population and slowing down the reaction.

Increasing the neutron speedThis option is incorrect. Control rods in a nuclear reactor are made of materials that absorbneutrons, such as boron or cadmium. By inserting the control rods into the reactor core, theabsorption of neutrons increases, which reduces the number of neutrons available for the chainreaction. Increasing neutron speed is not the purpose or function of control rods.

Increasing the electron speedThis option is also incorrect. Control rods in a nuclear reactor are not involved in controlling oraffecting the speed of electrons. The primary function of control rods is to regulate the number ofof neutrons available for the nuclear chain reaction, not to control electron behavior.

Decreasing the electron speedSimilar to Option C, control rods do not directly control or influence the speed of electrons in anuclear reactor. The control rods' primary purpose is to absorb neutrons and regulate the rate ofthe nuclear fission chain reaction, not to impact electron speed.

questions.explanation: The moderator helps slow down the neutrons produced by fission to sustain the chain reaction. Control rods can then be inserted into the reactor core to reduce the reaction rate or withdrawn to increase it. The heat created by fission turns the water into steam, which spins a turbine to produce carbon-free electricity. A rod, plate, or tube containing a material such as hafnium, boron, etc. is used to control the power of a nuclear reactor. By absorbing neutrons, a control rod prevents the neutrons from causing further fissions.

questions.text: Scattering is a process involving
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Scattering is not specifically related to reflection. Reflection refers to the bouncing back of light waves from a surface at the same angle of incidence. Scattering, on the other hand, involves the redirection of light waves in different directions due to interactions with particles or irregularities in a medium. Scattering can occur in various directions, not just at the same angle of incidence as in reflection.

This option is incorrect. Refraction refers to the bending of light waves as they pass from one medium to another with a different optical density. While refraction can cause changes in the direction and speed of light, it is not the primary process involved in scattering. Scattering is mainly associated with interactions between light and particles or irregularities in a medium.

This option is incorrect. Diffraction refers to the bending and spreading of waves around obstacles or through narrow openings. While both scattering and diffraction involve the redirection of waves, they are distinct processes. Scattering specifically involves the redirection of light waves due to interactions with particles or irregularities, whereas diffraction involves the bending and spreading of waves as they encounter obstacles or openings.

The correct answer is "None of these—scattering does not specifically involve reflection, refraction, or diffraction. Scattering refers to the process of redirecting or dispersing light or other forms of electromagnetic radiation when it interacts with particles or structures in its path. It is a phenomenon that occurs due to the interaction of light with various objects or media, such as molecules, dust particles, or irregularities in a medium.

questions.explanation: Scattering is, in physics, a change in the direction of motion of a particle because of a collision with another particle. As defined in physics, a collision can occur between particles that repel one another, such as two positive (or negative) ions and need not involve direct physical contact between the particles.

Solar radiation includes visible light, ultraviolet light, infrared, radio waves, X-rays, and gamma rays. Radiation is one way to transfer heat.

questions.text: If a nucleus with 198 atomic masses releases an alpha particle, its new mass becomes _.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: When a nucleus emits an alpha particle, these changes happen: The mass number decreases by 4 and the atomic number decreases by 2 So. 198-4 = 192

B suggests that the new mass of the nucleus becomes 202. However, this option is incorrect. When an alpha particle is released, the mass number of the nucleus decreases by 4. Therefore, the new mass would be lower than the original mass, not higher.

C suggests that the new mass of the nucleus becomes 200. This option is also incorrect. When an alpha particle is released, the mass number of the nucleus decreases by 4. Thus, if the original mass was 198, the new mass would be 198 minus 4 = 194, not 200.
D suggests that none of the given options are correct. While this option is technically correct, it does not provide a specific explanation.

questions.explanation: When a nucleus emits an alpha particle, these changes happen: the mass number decreases by 4, and the atomic number decreases by 2. So 198-4 gives 194.

questions.text: The quarks are fundamental particles. Which of the following statements is incorrect?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This statement is incorrect. Quarks do not possess fixed spin directions; their spins can be oriented in either direction, similar to other fundamental particles with spin.
This statement is correct. Quarks are classified as elementary particles because they are not made up of smaller constituents.

options.text: Quarks are constituents of protons and neutrons
options.explanation: This statement is correct. Quarks combine in specific configurations to form protons and neutrons, which are the building blocks of atomic nuclei.

options.text: Quarks cannot exist freely in nature
options.explanation: This statement is misleading. Quarks are always confined within larger particles (like protons and neutrons) due to the strong force, but it is more accurate to say they cannot be isolated in a free state.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is that quarks have fixed spin directions, which is incorrect. Quarks can have spins oriented in either direction. The other options accurately describe the properties of quarks: they are elementary particles, they combine to form protons and neutrons, and they cannot exist freely due to color confinement. While the statement about quarks not existing freely is technically true, it is important to note that they are confined by the strong force rather than being unable to exist at all.

Here's how to calculate the half-life:Relationship between mean life (τ) and half-life (t½):t½ = τ * ln(2)Convert mean life to seconds:τ = 200 s (already in seconds)Calculate half-life in seconds:t½ = 200 s * ln(2) t½ ≈ 200 s * 0.693 t½ ≈ 138.6 sConvert half-life to minutes:t½ ≈ 138.6 s * (1 min / 60 s) t½ ≈ 2.31 minutes

questions.text: The core of earth is hot due to _.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Fission reactions: The Earth's core contains radioactive elements like uranium, thorium, and potassium. These elements undergo radioactive decay, which is a type of nuclear fission. This process releases a tremendous amount of heat, making the core extremely hot.
Fusion reactions: Fusion reactions require extremely high temperatures and pressures, much higher than those found in the Earth's core. Fusion is the process that powers the sun and other stars.
Frictional forces: While some heat is generated by friction within the Earth, it's not the primary source of the core's heat.
All of these: Only fission reactions significantly contribute to the core's heat.

questions.text: How many milligrams of tritium will remain after 49.2 years if the starting amount is 32 mg? The half-life of tritium is 12.3 years.

questions.text: A body at temperature T radiates heat according to relation:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is not the correct relation for the radiation of heat by a body at temperature T. The correct relation is related to Stefan-Boltzmann's Law, which states that the total radiant power (heat) emitted by a black body per unit area is proportional to the fourth power of its absolute temperature (T). So, the correct relation is T4.

By the Stefan-Boltzmann Law, for a black body, the amount of heat radiated per unit area is directly proportional to the fourth power of the kelvin temperature. The proportionality constant is called Stefen’s constant and has a value of 5.67x10(-8).Option A,C and D are incorrect

questions.text: The radiations all around us are called _ radiations.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Natural radiations: Natural radiations refer to the various forms of radiation that occur in the environment without human intervention. These radiations come from natural sources like the Sun (which emits electromagnetic radiation, including visible light and ultraviolet radiation), cosmic rays from outer space, and radioactive elements found in rocks and soil. Some examples of natural radiation include sunlight, gamma rays from space, and radon gas emissions from the ground.

Artificial radiations: Artificial radiations are those that are produced by human activities. These radiations are not naturally present in the environment but are created through various processes and technologies. Some common sources of artificial radiation include X-rays and gamma rays used in medical imaging and radiation therapy, radio waves from communication devices like cell phones, and radioactive isotopes used in research, industry, and medicine.

Both A or B: This option suggests that the radiations around us can be either natural or artificial, depending on their source. As explained earlier, natural radiations come from natural sources, while artificial radiations are human-made.

No radiation is around us: This option is incorrect. Radiation is present all around us in various forms, as mentioned in options a) and b). Natural sources, as well as artificial sources, contribute to the radiation that we encounter in our daily lives.

questions.explanation: Humans come into contact with radiation on a daily basis. These radiations are both natural, such as the rays from the sun, and artificial, such as those from our TV screens or kitchen stoves. This radiation that we experience on a daily basis is known as background radiation

questions.text: The proton and antiproton collision will result in:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: a) Scattering: Scattering refers to the process where particles collide and change their direction due to interaction forces between them. In a proton-antiproton collision, there can be scattering events where the protons and antiprotons change their trajectories after the interaction. However, this is not the main outcome of the collision.

b) Repulsion: Repulsion occurs when particles with like charges (both positive or both negative) push each other away due to the electrostatic force. Since both protons and antiprotons carry a positive charge, they would experience repulsion if they come close to each other.

c) Attraction: Attraction happens when particles with opposite charges (positive and negative) are drawn toward each other due to the electrostatic force. In the case of a proton-antiproton collision, this is not the main outcome because their mutual annihilation dominates.

d) Annihilation: The correct answer is d) Annihilation. When a proton collides with an antiproton, they can undergo a process called annihilation. During this process, the mass of both particles is converted into energy according to Einstein's famous equation E=mc^2. The energy is released in the form of high-energy photons (gamma rays) or other particles. This annihilation process is an essential concept in particle physics and is used in experiments to study the properties of particles and their antiparticles.

questions.explanation: When a proton and antiproton collide with each other at relatively low speeds and energies, annihilation ensues, which is the process of a particle and its antiparticle colliding to produce photons.

questions.text: The wavelength of beta rays is measured by the _.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Interference is a phenomenon that occurs when two or more waves meet and combine. While beta rays can exhibit wave-like behavior and interfere with each other, interference is not a direct method of measuring their wavelength.
Polarization is the phenomenon where the oscillation of waves is restricted to a specific direction. It is not directly used for measuring the wavelength of beta rays.

Absorption is the process by which the energy of beta rays is absorbed by a material as they pass through it. While absorption spectroscopy can provide information about the energy levels of beta rays, it is not the primary method for measuring their wavelength.

Beta rays are high-speed electrons or positrons that are emitted from the nucleus during beta decay. These electrons or positrons have a characteristic wavelength associated with their motion, known as the de Broglie wavelength. Diffraction is a phenomenon in which waves bend around an obstacle and interfere with each other. When beta rays are diffracted through a crystal lattice, the diffraction pattern produced can be used to determine the wavelength of the beta rays. Therefore, the wavelength of beta rays is measured by diffraction, making option D the correct answer.

questions.explanation: Beta rays are high-speed electrons or positrons that are emitted from the nucleus during beta decay. These electrons or positrons have a characteristic wavelength associated with their motion, known as the de Broglie wavelength. Diffraction is a phenomenon in which waves bend around an obstacle and interfere with each other. When beta rays are diffracted through a crystal lattice, the diffraction pattern produced can be used to determine the wavelength of the beta rays. Therefore, the wavelength of beta rays is measured by diffraction, making option D the correct answer.

questions.text: The nuclear force is :
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: a) Spin independent: This option is incorrect. The nuclear force is not spin independent. It depends on the spins of the interacting nucleons (protons and neutrons).

b) Both charge and spin independent: This option is incorrect. The nuclear force is not charge independent. It depends on the charge of the interacting nucleons, i.e., protons and neutrons have different charges, and the nuclear force between them is influenced by this charge difference.

The nuclear force is nearly independent of whether the nucleons are neutrons or protons. This property is called charge independence.It depends on whether the spins of the nucleons are parallel or antiparallel, and has a noncentral or tensor component

d) Charge dependent: This option is incorrect. The nuclear force is charge independent. It depends on the charge of the interacting nucleons, as well as their spins. The nuclear force is a short-range force that acts between nucleons within the atomic nucleus.

questions.explanation: The nuclear force is nearly independent of whether the nucleons are neutrons or protons. This property is called charge independence.It depends on whether the spins of the nucleons are parallel or antiparallel and has a noncentral or tensor component.

questions.text: The deuterium atom has _ quarks

Correct. A deuterium atom consists of one proton and one neutron, and each of these contains three quarks:A proton has two up quarks and one down quark (uud).A neutron has one up quark and two down quarks (udd).So, the total number of quarks in deuterium is 3 (proton) + 3 (neutron) = 6 quarks.
Incorrect. This would imply the presence of three nucleons, but deuterium only has two nucleons (one proton and one neutron).
Incorrect. This number would correspond to four nucleons, which is not the case for deuterium.

questions.explanation: Correct. A deuterium atom consists of one proton and one neutron, and each of these contains three quarks:A proton has two up quarks and one down quark (uud).A neutron has one up quark and two down quarks (udd).So, the total number of quarks in deuterium is 3 (proton) + 3 (neutron) = 6 quarks.

questions.text: The spin of quarks is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: No, the spin of quarks is not 1. The spin of quarks is ½.

Spin is a quantum mechanical property of subatomic particles that are measured in units of "spin". The spin of a particle can be either an integer or a half-integer value. Quarks are a type of subatomic particle that has a spin of 1/2. This means that the spin of a quark can be oriented in one of two possible directions, either up or down. The spin of a quark is not 2, nor is it an integer value.

Spin is a quantum mechanical property of subatomic particles that is analogous to the angular momentum of a spinning object. In the case of quarks, their spin is a fundamental property that is intrinsic to their nature. The spin of a quark is always either +1/2 or -1/2, which means that it can be oriented in one of two possible directions. This property is what gives quarks their distinctive properties and allows them to interact with other particles in the way that they do.

questions.text: Radioactive material decays by simultaneous emission of two particles with half-lives of 1620 and 810 years. What is the time, in years, after which one-fourth of the material remains?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: According to the law of Rutherford - Soddy the number of atoms left after n number of Half-life is N=N0(½)nWhere N0 is the original number of atoms. The number of half-livesn=time of decay/effective half−lifeThe relation between effective disintegration constant(lambda) and half-life(T) is; λ=ln 2÷TNow, λ1+λ2=ln 2/T1+ln 2/T2and the effective half-life is ; 1/T=1/T1+1/T21/T=1/1620+1/8101/T=1+2/1620T=540Therefore, the number of half-lives is,n=time of decay/ effect half-life = t/540Now according to the question, N=N0(½)nTherefore,N/N0=(1/2)2 =(1/2)t/540(1/2)2=(1/2)t/5402 = t/540t=1080 years Therefore, the time in years, after which one-fourth of the material remains is 1080 years.

questions.text: What will be the product after the alpha decay of U238?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The alpha decay of uranium-238 (U-238) results in the formation of thorium-234 (Th-234) as the product.The decay of U-238 can be represented by the following equation:U-238 → Th-234 + He-4where He-4 is the alpha particle emitted during the decay process.Therefore, the product after alpha decay of U-238 is thorium-234 (Th-234).

Polonium-234 is not the correct product of the alpha decay of Uranium-238. While polonium is produced through the decay of uranium isotopes, in the case of U-238, the immediate daughter product after alpha decay is thorium-234, not polonium-234.

Radon-234 is not the correct product of the alpha decay of Uranium-238. Radon-222 is produced as an intermediate product in the decay chain of U-238, but it is not the immediate product resulting from the alpha decay of U-238.

This option suggests that none of the given options are correct. However, as explained above, option A (Th234) is indeed the correct product resulting from the alpha decay of U-238. Therefore, option D is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Option A is correct since Uranium-238 undergoes alpha decay to become Thorium-234. (The numbers following the chemical names refer to the number of protons plus neutrons.) In this reaction, Uranium-238 loses two protons and two neutrons to become the element Thorium-234.The alpha decay of uranium-238 (U-238) results in the formation of thorium-234 (Th-234) as the product.The decay of U-238 can be represented by the following equation:U-238 → Th-234 + He-4where He-4 is the alpha particle emitted during the decay process.Therefore, the product after alpha decay of U-238 is thorium-234 (Th-234).

questions.text: Which ion represents alpha particles?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: a) H+ (Hydrogen ion): This ion is a single proton, representing a hydrogen atom that has lost its electron. While it is positively charged, it does not represent an alpha particle.
b) C+ (Carbon ion): This ion is a positively charged carbon atom. Although it is an ion, it does not have the characteristics of an alpha particle.
c) Na+ (Sodium ion): This ion represents a sodium atom that has lost one electron. It is a common ion but does not represent an alpha particle.
d) He2+ (Helium ion): This is the correct answer as an alpha particle is essentially a helium nucleus, consisting of 2 protons and 2 neutrons, resulting in a +2 charge when it loses its electrons.

questions.explanation: Option D is correct because an alpha particle consists of 2 protons and 2 neutrons, which corresponds to a helium nucleus. When a helium atom loses its 2 electrons, it becomes a positively charged helium ion, represented as He2+. Options A (H+), B (C+), and C (Na+) are incorrect as they represent different ions (hydrogen, carbon, and sodium, respectively) and do not have the structure or charge of an alpha particle. Only the helium ion accurately reflects the composition of an alpha particle.

questions.text: The radioactive element when decays to the first half life the new element is called:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: a) Daughter element: This option suggests that when a radioactive element decays to its first half-life, the new element formed is called the daughter element. This is correct. During radioactive decay, the parent radioactive element transforms into a new element, known as the daughter element. The daughter element may itself be radioactive and undergo further decay.

b) Modified element: This option suggests that when a radioactive element decays to its first half-life, the new element formed is called the modified element. This is not a standard term used in the context of radioactive decay. The term used is "daughter element" as explained in option (a).

c) Radioactive element: This option suggests that when a radioactive element decays to its first half-life, it is still called a radioactive element. This is incorrect. The parent radioactive element transforms into a new element (the daughter element) during radioactive decay.

d) All of these: This option suggests that all the options (a), (b), and (c) are correct. This is not accurate. Only option (a) is correct. The new element formed during radioactive decay is called the daughter element.

questions.text: The radius R of a nucleus is given by:

options.explanation: Option A) R = roA^(-1/3) is incorrect because it suggests a negative exponent for the mass number, which would imply that the radius decreases as the mass number increases. However, in reality, the radius of a nucleus tends to increase with a higher mass number.

Correct Option: BThe radius of a nucleus can be approximated by the formula R = roA^(1/3), where ro is a constant and A represents the mass number of the nucleus. The mass number A corresponds to the total number of protons and neutrons in the nucleus.The formula suggests that the radius of a nucleus increases with the cube root of the mass number. This relationship arises from the idea that as the number of nucleons (protons and neutrons) increases, the overall size of the nucleus expands.

Option C) R = roA^3 is incorrect because it proposes a cubic relationship between the radius and the mass number, which does not align with the understanding that the radius increases with the cube root of the mass number.

questions.text: The radiations all around us are called _ radiations.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Natural radiations: Natural radiations refer to the various forms of radiation that occur in the environment without human intervention. These radiations come from natural sources like the Sun (which emits electromagnetic radiation, including visible light and ultraviolet radiation), cosmic rays from outer space, and radioactive elements found in rocks and soil. Some examples of natural radiation include sunlight, gamma rays from space, and radon gas emissions from the ground.

Artificial radiations: Artificial radiations are those that are produced by human activities. These radiations are not naturally present in the environment but are created through various processes and technologies. Some common sources of artificial radiation include X-rays and gamma rays used in medical imaging and radiation therapy, radio waves from communication devices like cell phones, and radioactive isotopes used in research, industry, and medicine.

questions.text: The angular momentum of a photon is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Infinite: The angular momentum of a photon cannot be infinite. This is because the angular momentum of a photon is quantized, which means that it can only have discrete values.

The angular momentum of a photon can be zero.The angular momentum of a photon is quantized, which means that it can only have discrete values. The allowed values of the angular momentum are multiples of ħ, where ħ is Planck's constant. The smallest possible value of the angular momentum of a photon is 0 ħ.A photon with zero angular momentum is called a scalar photon. Scalar photons have no spin, and they do not interact with magnetic fields.Scalar photons are still a subject of theoretical research, and they have not yet been experimentally observed. However, there is some evidence that they may exist. For example, some experiments have shown that light can be emitted or absorbed in discrete units, which is consistent with the existence of scalar photons.
Negative: The angular momentum of a photon cannot be negative. This is because the angular momentum of a photon is a vector quantity, and vector quantities cannot have negative values.

Still not found: The angular momentum of a photon has been experimentally observed, so it is not "still not found." However, the existence of scalar photons has not yet been experimentally confirmed, so it is possible that the angular momentum of a photon can be zero.

questions.explanation: The angular momentum of a photon can be zero.The angular momentum of a photon is quantized, which means that it can only have discrete values. The allowed values of the angular momentum are multiples of ħ, where ħ is Planck's constant. The smallest possible value of the angular momentum of a photon is 0 ħ.A photon with zero angular momentum is called a scalar photon. Scalar photons have no spin, and they do not interact with magnetic fields.Scalar photons are still a subject of theoretical research, and they have not yet been experimentally observed. However, there is some evidence that they may exist. For example, some experiments have shown that light can be emitted or absorbed in discrete units, which is consistent with the existence of scalar photons.

questions.text: The half life of U-238 against alpha decay is 4.5 x 109 years, find the activity of 1 kg of U-238:

questions.explanation: The number of atoms in 1kg of U-238 (N) = moles x Avagadro's constant = (mass/Mr) x Avagadro's constant = (1000/238) x 6.02x1023 = 2.529x1024Half-life = 4.5x109 yearsConverting this into seconds:(4.5x109) x (3.26x107)1.42x1017sA = λNWhere A is activity in Bq and λ is decay constantλ = 0.693/half-lifeA = (0.693/half-life)NA = (0693/1.42x1017)(2.529x1024)A = 1.234x107 BqConverting Bq to Ci1 Ci = 3.7x1010 BqA = 1.234x107/ 3.7x1010A = 3.34 x 10-4 Ci

questions.text: _ time is required for all atoms to decay.

options.explanation: If the statement says that "infinite time is required for all atoms to decay," it means that the radioactive substance is stable, and the decay process never completes. The decay curve of a radioactive sample is exponential i.e. it tends towards zero but never reaches zero. An infinite time would be required for it to reach zero. Hence Option B is correct.

questions.text: Sunlight consists of:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Sunlight does indeed contain infrared radiation, which is electromagnetic radiation with longer wavelengths than visible light. Infrared radiation is responsible for warmth and is often felt as heat when exposed to sunlight. So, option A is correct.

Sunlight also contains ultraviolet (UV) radiation, which is electromagnetic radiation with shorter wavelengths than visible light. UV radiation from the sun can have both beneficial and harmful effects on living organisms, such as aiding in the synthesis of vitamin D in humans but also causing sunburn and increasing the risk of skin cancer. So, option B is correct.
This option correctly states that sunlight consists of infrared rays and UV rays, along with visible light. So, option C is correct.
While the sun does emit gamma rays as part of its electromagnetic spectrum, gamma rays are a significant component of sunlight that reaches the Earth's surface.

questions.explanation: The Sun emits radiation right across the electromagnetic spectrum, from extremely high-energy X-rays to ultra-long-wavelength radio waves, and everything in-between. The peak of this emission occurs in the visible portion of the spectrum.Different wavelengths of light generally come from different regions of the Sun’s atmosphere or are due to particular atoms radiating at specific wavelengths (spectral emission lines). Visible light, for example, comes from the photosphere (or surface) whereas most infrared light comes from the lower chromosphere just above. Much of the high-energy UV and X-ray photons come from the Sun’s outer atmosphere.

questions.text: Which type of radiation do explosions give off?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Explosions involving nuclear reactions, such as nuclear fission or fusion, can release a significant amount of neutron radiation. Neutron radiation is a type of ionizing radiation consisting of free neutrons. These neutrons can penetrate materials and cause nuclear reactions in other substances, leading to further radiation emissions. So, option A is correct in certain explosive scenarios involving nuclear processes.

Alpha radiation consists of alpha particles, which are helium-4 nuclei consisting of two protons and two neutrons. While alpha radiation is not typically emitted directly by explosions, certain explosive events involving nuclear reactions can produce alpha-emitting radionuclides as byproducts. However, it is not a primary type of radiation emitted by explosions in general. So, option B is less common but possible in specific cases involving radioactive materials.

Explosions, especially those involving nuclear reactions or high-energy processes, can produce gamma radiation. Gamma radiation is electromagnetic radiation with high energy and short wavelengths. It is often emitted alongside other forms of radiation during nuclear events. So, option C is correct for many explosive scenarios involving nuclear or energetic processes.

Explosions can indeed give off neutron radiation, alpha radiation (though less commonly), and gamma radiation, depending on the nature of the explosion and the materials involved. So, option D is correct in the broadest sense, encompassing various types of explosions and their potential radiation emissions.

questions.explanation: The release of radiation is a phenomenon unique to nuclear explosions. There are several kinds of radiation emitted; these types include gamma, neutron, and ionizing radiation, and are emitted not only at the time of detonation (initial radiation) but also for long periods of time afterward (residual radiation).

questions.text: The half-life of sodium-24 is _ which is used to estimate the volume of blood in a patient.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Option A (6 hours) is incorrect because the half-life of sodium-24 is not this short; it's longer.

Sodium-24 is a radioactive isotope of sodium with a half-life of approximately 15 hours. This means that if a sample of sodium-24 contains 1000 atoms, after 15 hours, only 500 atoms will remain.The radioactive decay of sodium-24 can be used to estimate the volume of blood in a patient. This involves injecting a small amount of sodium chromate, which contains sodium-24, into the patient's bloodstream. As the sodium-24 decays, it is eliminated from the body through the urine. By measuring the rate at which sodium-24 is excreted in the urine, the volume of blood in the patient's body can be estimated.
Option C (8 hours) is incorrect because the half-life of sodium-24 is not this short; it's longer.
Option D (15 days) is incorrect because the half-life of sodium-24 is not this long; it's much shorter.

questions.text: Which of the following is the unit of absorbed dose?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The Sievert (Sv) is the unit of equivalent dose, which takes into account the biological effects of different types of radiation on the human body. It's not the unit of absorbed dose.

Absorbed dose is a measure of the amount of energy deposited in a material by ionizing radiation per unit mass. The unit of absorbed dose is the gray (Gy), which is defined as the absorption of one joule of radiation energy per kilogram of the material.The sievert (Sv) is a unit of dose equivalent, which takes into account the biological effect of different types of ionizing radiation on human tissue. The roentgen (R) is a unit of exposure, which measures the ionization produced by X-rays or gamma rays in air. The curie (Ci) is a unit of activity, which measures the rate of radioactive decay of a substance.
The Roentgen (R) is a unit of exposure to ionizing radiation, specifically for X-rays and gamma rays. It measures the amount of ionization produced in the air, not absorbed dose.
The Curie (Ci) is a unit of radioactive decay, specifically the activity of a radioactive substance. It's not a unit of absorbed dose, which measures the energy deposited by radiation in a material.

questions.explanation: Absorbed dose is a measure of the amount of energy deposited in a material by ionizing radiation per unit mass. The roentgen (R) is a unit of exposure, which measures the ionization produced by X-rays or gamma rays in air. The curie (Ci) is a unit of activity, which measures the rate of radioactive decay of a substance.

questions.text: The isotope of Iodine-131 is used in the treatment of:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Iodine-131 is not used to treat blood cancers, as these cancers primarily involve the bone marrow and blood cells, and Iodine-131 targets thyroid tissue.
Iodine-131 is not used to treat bone cancers. Bone cancers originate in the bone tissue itself, while Iodine-131 is mainly utilized to treat thyroid conditions.
Iodine-131 is not effective in treating lung tumors. Lung tumors are distinct from thyroid tissue, which is the target for Iodine-131 therapy.

Iodine-131 is a radioactive isotope of iodine that is used in the treatment of thyroid cancer. The thyroid gland absorbs iodine to produce thyroid hormones, and cancerous thyroid cells also absorb iodine. When iodine-131 is taken orally or intravenously, it is absorbed by the thyroid cells and emits radiation, which destroys the cancerous cells.Iodine-131 is preferred over other treatments for thyroid cancer because it is highly effective and has a low risk of damaging surrounding tissues. It is also relatively easy to administer and does not require hospitalization.

questions.explanation: Thyroid glands absorb nearly all of the iodine in your body. Because of this, radioactive iodine ‘I-131’ can be used to treat thyroid cancer. Where the radiation can destroy the thyroid gland and any other thyroid cells (including cancer cells) that take up iodine, with little effect on the rest of your body.

questions.text: Which of the following has maximum ionizing power?

options.explanation: Alpha particles have the highest ionization power than the other two types of radiation owing to the fact that an 𝝰-particle is a doubly ionized helium nucleus, i.e. He+2. 𝞫-particles are simply electrons (e-1), They have moderate ionizing power and 𝝲-rays are uncharged EM radiation and have the lowest ionizing power among all. Therefore, out of the three, 𝝰-rays have the most electric charge and are the most massive. Hence, when they are passed through a substance, they tend to ionize the substance (by attracting and stripping off the electrons from it) than 𝞫-and 𝝲-radiation. X-rays are also a part of EM radiation, but still cannot ionize as powerful as 𝝰-rays. Whereas neutrons have no charge and hence no ionization power.

questions.text: In water pipes _ is used to detect leakages.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Radiation, specifically gamma radiation or radioactive tracers, is effectively used to detect leaks in water pipes. The radiation passes through the pipe and can highlight areas where water is escaping.

Light is not suitable for detecting leaks in water pipes because water is transparent to light, making it ineffective for revealing the presence of leaks. Light cannot penetrate or reveal leaks in non-transparent materials like pipes.

Electrical current is not used to detect leaks in water pipes as it is not sensitive enough to detect small changes caused by leaks. The presence of water does not significantly affect the flow of current in such systems.

options.text: None of these options are correct
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because radiation is indeed used to detect leaks in water pipes.

questions.explanation: Radiation is used to detect leakages in water pipes due to its ability to penetrate materials and reveal hidden defects. Specifically, radioactive tracers can be introduced into the water system, and their movement can be tracked with specialized detectors to identify leak locations. This method provides accurate results in pinpointing leaks.Other options such as light and current are ineffective for this purpose. Light cannot reveal leaks in opaque materials like pipes, and current doesn't change significantly with the presence of leaks, making these methods unsuitable for leak detection.

questions.text: In medical diagnosis for precise internal imaging of brain, _ radiographs are used.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: X-rays can be a common imaging test for the brain that uses small amounts of high-energy electromagnetic radiation to produce images for doctors to view the inside of the body. The level of exposure is considered safe for adults. A:X rays are used

Beta radiation is used to treat eye and bone cancer. The eye is a very delicate part of the body, and that is why low energy electric rays are used in treatment. The best advantage of beta radiation when it comes to eye treatment is that 3⁄4 of the rays are absorbed within the first 2mm. The remaining is absorbed within the next one mm.

gamma rays ionize living tissue, causing cancer by producing free radicals. However, because gamma rays also kill bacteria and cancer cells, they have been used to kill certain types of cancer. In a controlled procedure, gamma rays are employed as a “gamma knife” consisting of multiple concentrated beams of gamma rays that are focused directly onto a tumor to kill the cancer cells while leaving the surrounding cells unharmed. Gamma rays have also been used to sterilize equipment as an alternative to chemical treatments

Alpha radiation is used to treat various forms of cancer. This process, called unsealed source radiotherapy, involves inserting tiny amounts of radium-226 into cancerous masses. The alpha particles destroy cancer cells but lack the penetrating ability to damage the surrounding healthy cells. Radium-226 has mostly been replaced by Safer, more effective radiation sources, such as cobalt-60. Xofigo, the brand name of Radium-223, is still used to treat bone cancer.

questions.explanation: For internal imaging of braina X-Ray radiographs are used. Some examples include use of X-rays in CAT Scans and MRIs.

questions.text: When a gamma photon is entered into the nucleus it:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because the entry of a gamma photon into a nucleus typically leads to the excitation of the nucleus. De-excitation occurs after excitation when the nucleus returns to a lower energy state by emitting gamma rays or other particles. However, the initial interaction of the gamma photon results in the excitation of the nucleus, not de-excitation.

When a gamma photon enters a nucleus, it can excite the nucleus.Gamma photons are high-energy photons that carry no electric charge. When they interact with a nucleus, they can transfer energy to the nucleus. This energy transfer can result in the excitation of the nucleus to a higher energy state.In an excited state, the nucleus may undergo subsequent de-excitation by emitting gamma rays or other particles to return to a lower energy state. However, the initial interaction of the gamma photon with the nucleus leads to the excitation of the nucleus.

Gamma photons have high energy and penetrate matter easily, including the nucleus. They do not experience significant scattering interactions with the nucleus. Scattering generally occurs when photons interact with electrons or other charged particles. Therefore, this option is incorrect.

This option is incorrect because gamma photons can indeed interact with the nucleus. They carry enough energy to excite the nucleus to higher energy states. The interaction between gamma photons and the nucleus is important for nuclear processes such as nuclear excitation, de-excitation, and emission of gamma rays. Therefore, the statement that there is no effect on the nucleus is incorrect.

questions.explanation: In this case, a gamma photon imparts its energy and transfers its energy to an atomic electron causing the ejection of an electron from that nucleus.

questions.text: In water pipes _ is used to detect leakages.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Radioisotope tracer technology is widely used to detect leakage of process pipe and heat exchanger system pipe that carries liquid and gas in the oil and gas industry. The same technique is also used to identify leaks in water pipes under the ground.

b) Light: This option is used in some leak detection systems. Fiber optic sensors or laser-based systems can be used to detect changes in light intensity caused by water leakage. However, this method might not be suitable for all types of pipes and conditions.

c) Current: This option is not commonly used for detecting leakages in water pipes. While changes in electrical conductivity can indicate the presence of water, other methods like pressure changes or acoustic sensing are usually more practical for leak detection.

d) Heat: This option is sometimes used for leak detection. In some cases, thermal imaging or infrared cameras can detect temperature changes caused by water leaks. Water absorbs and conducts heat differently than surrounding materials, allowing for the identification of leaks.

questions.explanation: Radioisotope tracer technology is widely used to detect leakage of process pipe and heat exchanger system pipe that carries liquid and gas in the oil and gas industry. The same technique is also used to identify leaks in water pipes under the ground.

questions.text: The Uranium nucleus 23892U emits an alpha-particle to become thorium, which then emits a beta-particle to become protactinium. What is the proton number (atomic number) of protactinium?

options.explanation: Uranium has an atomic number of 92. When it emits an alpha particle consisting of two protons and two neutrons, the resulting nucleus will have an atomic number that is reduced by 2. Therefore, the atomic number of the resulting thorium nucleus will be 92 - 2 = 90, not 89.

As explained above, the emission of an alpha particle reduces the atomic number by 2. Therefore, the atomic number of thorium would be 92 - 2 = 90. However, the question asks for the atomic number of protactinium, not thorium.

After thorium emits a beta particle, it undergoes beta decay, where a neutron is converted into a proton. This increases the atomic number by 1. Therefore, the atomic number of protactinium would be 90 + 1 = 91.

Then, thorium emits a beta particle, which increases the atomic number from 90 to 91, resulting in protactinium. Therefore, the atomic number of protactinium is 91. Option C is correct. The other options do not accurately reflect the atomic number changes due to the sequence of decay processes described in the question.

questions.text: Cosmic radiations come from
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: While radioactive materials can emit radiation, cosmic radiation is not solely derived from radioactive materials. Cosmic radiation consists of high-energy particles that originate from sources outside the Earth.

The Earth's crust does contain some radioactive elements, but cosmic radiation is not primarily sourced from the Earth's crust. It originates from sources outside the Earth, such as the Sun, distant stars, and other celestial objects.

Nuclear stations generate nuclear radiation, but cosmic radiation is not directly related to nuclear stations. Cosmic radiation is a natural phenomenon that is independent of human-made nuclear facilities.

This is the correct answer. Cosmic radiation refers to high-energy particles, such as protons, electrons, and even atomic nuclei, that originate from outer space. These particles can come from various sources, including the Sun, other stars, supernovae, and distant galaxies.

questions.explanation: Cosmic radiation is comprised of energetic particles that emanate from outer space. These particles include protons, electrons, and atomic nuclei, and they originate from sources such as the Sun, other stars, supernovae, and galaxies far beyond our solar system. In contrast, terrestrial radioactive materials and nuclear stations on Earth do not contribute to cosmic radiation. Likewise, while the Earth's crust contains radioactive elements, they are not the source of cosmic radiation. Thus, the correct answer is outer space.

questions.text: In the given diagram, different parts of a nuclear reactor are shown.Which of the following correctly identifies the labeled part "Q" in the above diagram?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The core is the central region of a nuclear reactor where the nuclear fission reaction takes place. It contains fuel elements, typically made of fissile material such as uranium or plutonium, arranged in a specific configuration. The core generates heat through nuclear fission, which is then used to produce steam and generate electricity. Hence Q is not the core.

The moderator is a material used in a nuclear reactor to slow down fast neutrons produced during the fission process. Slowing down the neutrons increases the likelihood of their interaction with the fuel, allowing a sustained chain reaction to occur. Commonly used moderators include water, heavy water (deuterium oxide), and graphite. Hence, Q is the moderator.

Control rods are rods or blades made of materials such as boron or cadmium that can absorb neutrons. They are used to control the rate of the nuclear reaction by adjusting the number of neutrons available to sustain the chain reaction. By inserting or withdrawing control rods into the core, the reactivity of the reactor can be controlled, ensuring a safe and controlled operation. Hence, Q is not the control rod.

Fissile uranium refers to uranium isotopes, such as uranium-235 (U-235), that can undergo nuclear fission upon absorbing a neutron. Fissile uranium plays a crucial role in nuclear reactors as the fuel source for sustaining the chain reaction. U-235 is the most commonly used fissile isotope in nuclear reactors, although other isotopes like plutonium-239 can also be used. Hence, Q is not the fissile uranium.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Moderator. The moderator is essential in a nuclear reactor for slowing down fast neutrons, which increases the probability of further fission events, thus sustaining the chain reaction. This is distinct from the core, which primarily houses the fuel and is the site of fission, control rods, which regulate the reaction rate by absorbing neutrons, and fissile uranium, which is the actual fuel undergoing fission. Therefore, option B accurately identifies the function of the labeled part 'Q'.

questions.text: The X in the given nuclear equation is 94Be+42He—>126C + X
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: If X represents a proton, it would imply that the number of protons on both sides of the equation is equal. However, this violates the conservation of atomic number since the atomic number of beryllium (Be) is 4, helium (He) is 2, and carbon (C) is 6. Hence, option A is incorrect.

The X represents an unknown particle or element that is not specified in the equation. The equation shows a nuclear reaction where beryllium (Be) reacts with helium (He) to produce carbon (C), and there is another particle represented by X involved in the reaction. To determine the identity of X, we need to consider the conservation laws in nuclear reactions. In this case, the atomic number (proton number) and mass number must be conserved. If X represents a neutron, it would imply that the number of neutrons on both sides of the equation is equal. Neutrons do not affect the atomic number, so this option satisfies the conservation laws. Based on the conservation laws and the absence of indications for specific particle emissions or interactions, the most reasonable option is that X represents a neutron, which satisfies the conservation of atomic and mass numbers. Therefore, option B (neutron) is the correct answer.
Positrons are positively charged electrons. Since the equation does not involve any annihilation or positron emission, it is unlikely that X represents a positron. Hence, option C is incorrect.
Electrons are negatively charged particles. Since the equation does not involve any beta decay or electron capture, it is unlikely that X represents an electron. Hence, option D is incorrect.

questions.explanation: In the given nuclear reaction, 94Be + 42He → 126C + X, we need to identify particle X. To satisfy the conservation of atomic and mass numbers, X must be a neutron. The atomic numbers already balance (4 + 2 = 6), and the mass numbers on the left side (9 + 4 = 13) require an additional 1 on the right side to match the 12 from carbon, making a neutron the only viable option.Options A (proton), C (positron), and D (electron) do not satisfy both conservation laws in this reaction, as they either introduce charge imbalances or do not account for the necessary mass number.

questions.text: Which of the following particles experience the strongest nuclear force?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Quarks are elementary particles that makeup hadrons, such as protons and neutrons. Quarks do experience the strong nuclear force, which is the strongest of the four fundamental forces in nature. Therefore, quarks do experience a strong nuclear force but not more than Hadrons. Hence, option A is incorrect.

Leptons are another class of elementary particles, which include electrons and neutrinos. Leptons do not directly experience the strong nuclear force. They interact primarily through the electromagnetic and weak nuclear forces. Therefore, leptons do not experience the strongest nuclear force.

Photons are elementary particles that mediate the electromagnetic force. They do not directly experience the strong nuclear force. Photons are involved in electromagnetic interactions, such as the transmission of light and electromagnetic radiation. Therefore, photons do not experience the strongest nuclear force.

Hadrons are composite particles made up of quarks. They include particles like protons and neutrons. Hadrons do experience a strong nuclear force, which binds the quarks together within the hadrons. Therefore, hadrons experience the strongest nuclear force. In summary, the correct option is d (hadrons). Hadrons, being composed of quarks, experience the strong nuclear force, which is the strongest of the fundamental forces.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is option D: Hadrons. Hadrons are composite particles composed of quarks held together by the strong nuclear force, which is the strongest of the four fundamental forces. This force acts between quarks, binding them to form hadrons like protons and neutrons. While quarks do experience the strong force, it is the hadrons that display this force as the most significant interaction, making them the particles that experience the strongest nuclear force. Leptons and photons do not interact via the strong nuclear force, and thus they do not experience it as hadrons do.

questions.text: Which of the following is the region with the greatest stability of a nucleon?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: In a binding energy per nucleon graph, the region with the greatest stability of a nucleon is the region where the binding energy per nucleon is at its highest. The binding energy per nucleon represents the energy required to remove a single nucleon from the nucleus. Typically, the region with the greatest stability corresponds to the peak or plateau in the binding energy per nucleon graph. This region indicates the nuclei with the highest binding energy per nucleon, which means they are the most tightly bound and stable. Nuclei in this region tend to have a moderate mass number (around B = 50-100) and are often referred to as the "peak of stability" or the "most tightly bound" nuclei. They are typically found in the middle of the periodic table and include elements like iron (Fe), nickel (Ni), and xenon (Xe).Hence, option A is the incorrect answer.

This peak indicates the nuclei that are the most tightly bound and stable. Nuclei with moderate mass numbers, such as iron (Fe) and nickel (Ni), possess the highest binding energy per nucleon and are located in the middle of the periodic table. Therefore, option B is correct.Option A is incorrect because light nuclei have lower binding energy per nucleon. Option C and D are incorrect because heavy nuclei and those with very high atomic numbers are less stable due to their lower binding energy per nucleon compared to the peak region.

questions.explanation: To solve this problem, we first determine how many half-lives have passed in 15 days. Given the half-life of 5 days, 15 days corresponds to 3 half-lives. For each half-life, the remaining quantity of the radioactive substance is halved. Therefore, after 3 half-lives, the remaining quantity is (1/2)³ = 1/8 of the original amount. This means 7/8 of the original amount has decayed. To convert this into a percentage, we calculate (7/8) * 100 = 87.5%. Thus, the probability of decay after 15 days is 87.5%, making Option B the correct choice. The other options do not accurately reflect the decay over three half-lives and are therefore incorrect.

questions.text: An atom would be neutral if it has equal number of:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: In an atom, there are two types of charged particles: protons and electrons. Protons have a positive charge (+1), while electrons have a negative charge (-1). For an atom to be neutral, it must have an equal number of protons and electrons. This is because the positive charge of the protons cancels out the negative charge of the electrons, resulting in a net charge of zero.

"Uncharged particles" is not a term typically used in the context of atomic structure. Atoms are composed of protons, neutrons, and electrons. Protons and electrons have charges, as explained in option a), while neutrons are neutral particles with no charge.

Neutrons are subatomic particles found in the nucleus of an atom. They have no electrical charge, meaning they are electrically neutral. Neutrons play a crucial role in the stability of the nucleus, and their presence does not affect the overall charge of the atom. Since they are neutral, they do not contribute to the atom's charge, and an atom remains neutral as long as it has an equal number of protons and electrons.

options.text: All of these options are correct
options.explanation: This option is incorrect. While neutrons are indeed neutral particles, the term "uncharged particles" is not used to describe the particles in an atom. The correct term for neutral particles is "neutrons."

questions.explanation: An atom is neutral if it has an equal number of protons and electrons which are charged particles. Neutrons are not charged particles.

questions.text: A nucleus emits an α- particle, followed by two β- particles. The final nucleus will be:
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: An isotone of the original one
options.explanation: An isotone is a nucleus that has the same number of neutrons as another nucleus, but a different number of protons.

options.text: An isotope of the original one
options.explanation: The final nucleus will be an isotope of the original one.An alpha particle is a helium nucleus, which has 2 protons and 2 neutrons. When an alpha particle is emitted, the nucleus loses 2 protons and 2 neutrons. Two beta particles are electrons, which have no protons or neutrons. When two beta particles are emitted, the nucleus gains 2 electrons, but no protons or neutrons.The net effect of emitting an alpha particle and two beta particles is to reduce the number of protons by 2, but not to change the number of neutrons. Therefore, the final nucleus will have the same number of neutrons as the original nucleus, but two fewer protons. This makes the final nucleus an isotope of the original one.

options.text: An isobar of the original one
options.explanation: An isobar is a nucleus that has the same mass number as another nucleus, but a different number of protons and neutrons.
None of these options applies to the final nucleus, which has both a different number of protons and neutrons than the original nucleus.

questions.text: Half-life of radioactive element means:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Option (a) "Full life decay" is not correct. The term "full life decay" is not a standard concept in the context of radioactive decay. The correct term is "half-life," which refers to the time it takes for half of the radioactive atoms in a sample to decay.

Option (b) "Quarter life decay" is also not correct. The concept of "quarter life decay" is not used in radioactive decay. The relevant term is still "half-life," which indicates the time taken for half of the radioactive atoms to decay.

options.text: Time for half of the element to decay
options.explanation: Half life is the time that it takes for half of the original value of some amount of a radioactive element to decay. This also implies that one half life is the time that it takes for the activity of a source to fall to half its original value.

questions.text: If a person is irradiated accidently by high radiation, what immediate effects would be seen?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: In the case of high radiation exposure, the skin can be affected, and skin burning may occur. This is known as radiation burns or radiation dermatitis. The severity of the burns depends on the dose and duration of exposure. Radiation can damage skin cells, leading to redness, blistering, and peeling of the skin.

High blood pressure is not an immediate effect of radiation exposure. While high doses of radiation can have long-term effects on the cardiovascular system, such as an increased risk of heart disease, it typically does not cause an immediate rise in blood pressure following exposure.
Fever is also not an immediate effect of radiation exposure. Fever is generally associated with infections or inflammation, not directly caused by exposure to radiation.

options.text: Any of these symptoms can be seen
options.explanation: Being exposed to a lot of radiation over a short period of time, such as from a radiation emergency, can cause skin burns. It may also lead to acute radiation syndrome (ARS, or "radiation sickness"). The symptoms of ARS include headache and diarrhea. They usually start within hours

questions.text: The radiations all around us are called _ radiations.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Natural radiations: Natural radiations refer to the various forms of radiation that occur in the environment without human intervention. These radiations come from natural sources like the Sun (which emits electromagnetic radiation, including visible light and ultraviolet radiation), cosmic rays from outer space, and radioactive elements found in rocks and soil. Some examples of natural radiation include sunlight, gamma rays from space, and radon gas emissions from the ground.

Artificial radiations: Artificial radiations are those that are produced by human activities. These radiations are not naturally present in the environment but are created through various processes and technologies. Some common sources of artificial radiation include X-rays and gamma rays used in medical imaging and radiation therapy, radio waves from communication devices like cell phones, and radioactive isotopes used in research, industry, and medicine.

Both A or B: This option suggests that the radiations around us can be either natural or artificial, depending on their source. As explained earlier, natural radiations come from natural sources, while artificial radiations are human-made.

No radiation is around us: This option is incorrect. Radiation is present all around us in various forms, as mentioned in options a) and b). Natural sources, as well as artificial sources, contribute to the radiation that we encounter in our daily lives.

questions.explanation: Humans come into contact with radiation on a daily basis. These radiations are both natural, such as the rays from the sun, and artificial, such as those from our TV screens or kitchen stoves. This radiation that we experience on a daily basis is known as background radiation

Half life :The half-life of a radioactive isotope is the amount of time it takes for one-half of the radioactive isotope to decay.
